full_text:
Good Data Management

Data Integrity Expectations

Data integrity ensures processes are in place to assure data is Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original and Accurate, Complete, Consistent, Enduring and Available (ALCOA-CCEA). Systems and processes should be in place to prevent situations that might impact data integrity and in turn the robustness of decisions based upon those data.

Questions align to the principles of ALCOA CCEA

Your Company Legal Entity Name | University of Helsinki
Your Name | Dr. Anniina Färkkilä
Date completed | 11.1.2021

No. | Data Integrity Expectations | Yes/No | Description of how the data integrity expectations will be met
1 | Will all experiment write-ups have a unique ID and a clearly documented purpose? | Yes | 
2 | Will all experimental methodologies including equipment and material details be fully documented? | Yes | 
3 | Will information about the test material be sufficient to ensure its identification? E.g. Compound number | Yes | 
4 | Will data be recorded accurately and legibly?
If data are not located in a lab notebook, is there a suitable alternative? 
If yes, is there a reference to its location? | Yes
Yes 
Yes | All the data are stored digitally in a hard drive and secured University servers.
5 | Where data are observational and require subjective assessment, will systems be in place to avoid bias?
For studies involving animals will measures such as randomisation and blinding be used to minimise/avoid bias? | Yes
n/a | Whenever possible all the data will be validated using non-biased computational methods or analysis by multiple researchers.
6 | Will statistical methods and/or any software used be fully recorded? E.g. Method names, software & version numbers | Yes | 
7 | Will any selective use of data for inclusion and/or exclusion be fully justified and recorded? E.g.

ed be fully recorded? E.g. Method names, software & version numbers | Yes | 
7 | Will any selective use of data for inclusion and/or exclusion be fully justified and recorded? E.g. removal of statistically justified outliers | Yes | Inclusion / exclusion of the data outliers will be discussed in the lab notebook and the research article / report.
8 | Will data (paper or electronic) be collected into an appropriate long term secure location? Please describe how this is this managed, for example, 
Please describe document retention policies
Please describe processes to ensure paper and electronic data is retained and secured
Please describe any processes for data transfer, if applicable | Yes | All the paper data and documents are stored in a locked cabinet at the laboratory. In addition, they are also stored in electronic form in a University server with restricted access. Data transfer can be enabled via access to secured University server.
9 | Will data trails exist so that conclusions can be traced back to raw data (enabling reconstruction of experiments)? | Yes | Links to all the raw data are stored electronically at the University server.
10 | Will any of the work for this engagement/contract be sub-contracted to other 3rd parties?
If yes, how will you ensure the integrity of the data generated by the sub-contractor?

0 | Will any of the work for this engagement/contract be sub-contracted to other 3rd parties?
If yes, how will you ensure the integrity of the data generated by the sub-contractor? | Yes/No | Some of the work will be carried out by using the internal University of Helsinki core facility services.
